text: The surgeon uses an atraumatic grasper through a five millimeter port to retract the gallbladder over the liver during a multi-port cholecystectomy.
The operation employs the Endoscope Plus camera in sensitive mode, displaying a concentrated fluorescent green visualization of ICG permeated through the tissue against a black and white background.

The surgeon separates the cystic artery from the gallbladder wall and dissects the space above the artery to expose the critical view. The Firefly imaging system is activated to enhance visualization within the triangle of Calot, aiding in identifying anatomical structures in cases with complex or barren anatomy.

The surgeon uses the Firefly imaging system to review the exposed critical view, clearly identifying the cystic duct, common bile duct, and common hepatic duct. The bifurcation of the left and right hepatic ducts is also visible.
The surgeon applies clips to the cystic artery and cystic duct, followed by transection of these structures using electrocautery.
The surgeon inspects the dissection bed before removing the gallbladder from the fossa, confirming the absence of any visible aberrancies or ductal Luschka leaks.
